The Story of Village Palampur is a chapter in the IX Economics curriculum covering production and resources.
This topic discusses land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship as the four factors of production.
This topic explains how land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship rely on each other for production.
This topic analyzes the relationship between agricultural work and other economic activities in villages.
This chapter explores human beings as the most productive resource in an economy.
This chapter examines the causes and consequences of poverty in India.
This chapter discusses the mechanisms ensuring food availability for all citizens.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Quality of population and government schemes

This topic assesses the success of anti-poverty programs and strategies.
This topic explores the correlation between educational attainment and economic status.
This topic details the Public Distribution System and its role in food security.
This topic analyzes the agricultural changes and economic effects resulting from technological advancements.

excluded_content: Chapter 5 - Pastoralists in the Modern World (IX) is assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
Chapter 1 - The Story of Village Palampur (IX) is to be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
Natural Vegetation and Wildlife map pointing (IX) is evaluated in annual examination, content not specified for board exam marks distribution explicitly beyond the note.
The Age of Industrialisation (X) is to be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
Lifelines of National Economy map pointing (X) is evaluated in Board Examination.
teacher_notes: Refer to NCF-2023 pages 320-323 for Aims & Objectives details.
Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September; carryover of project to home is strictly avoided.
Projects should use eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.
Project records (internal assessment) should be kept for three months from the date of declaration of result for verification.
Maps available on Govt. of India website may be used.
Items of locating and labeling may also be given for identification.
For visually impaired candidates, project work can culminate in suitable formats like skits, albums, songs, etc.
